method: 4 decorrelated lenses (ADR-017 fidelity, implementability, containment/escape, gap completeness) each in its own git worktree and instructed to DRIVE REAL PATHS, + an independent skeptic per finding with no default verdict, also in a worktree. Read probe first (passed).
result: 16 raised, 13 CONFIRMED (7 major, 4 minor, 2 info), 3 REFUTED.
verdict_on_the_adr: ADR-021 is NOT salvageable by patching. D1s seam is architecturally wrong: Rejects(id SourceIdentity) carries no kind, so it cannot express ADR-017:396-401s typed own-source carve-out. A skeptic probe implementing D1+D3+D4 verbatim against the real mc/boundary code rejected ALL SEVEN typed grants (/mc/session, /home/agent, /mc/records/output, /mc/workflow, /mc/private/completion-seal, /mc/attachments/in, /workspace/operator/traces) => no container could ever launch, while the ADRs own test list stayed green because it pins only the deny side.

raised.title: D1's `Rejects(id SourceIdentity)` cannot express ADR-017's typed own-source carve-out; D3 and Sharp-edge 2 defer it to "D5's per-class check", which does not exist
raised.claim: ADR-021 D3 makes the whole MC_HOME tree one protected root and D1 gives the predicate the signature `func (j Jurisdiction) Rejects(id SourceIdentity) error` — an identity-only parameter. ADR-017:376-380 excepts "each exact typed own-source grant in Decisions 4, 6, and 8" from the MC_HOME union, and :396-401 requires "the exact own session, derived own state, selected runtime-control directory, or generated projection MAY be inside MC_HOME, but any sibling/ancestor/other identity is still denied." `Rejects(id)` receives nothing that names which grant the caller claims, so it cannot distinguish own-session from other-session: it must reject both. D3 and Sharp edge 2 both route this to "the typed-grant carve-out (D5)" / "D5's per-class check" — but D5 is titled "HOME is injected, not ambient" and contains no per-class check, no carve-out, and no mention of typed sources. No decision in ADR-021 defines the mechanism. ADR-017 wins on conflict, so ADR-021 as written is unimplementable for the sources D1's rationale #1 says most need it.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021:57-58: `// Callable WITHOUT an allow-root match — typed system sources need exactly this.` / `func (j Jurisdiction) Rejects(id SourceIdentity) error` — the only parameter is an identity.
ADR-021:136-138 (D3): "The enumeration is kept anyway, because it is not redundant in the **descendant** direction for the typed-grant carve-out (D5): a typed source must be checked against the exact class it claims, not merely \"inside `MC_HOME`\"."
ADR-021:179 (D5 heading): "### D5. HOME is injected, not ambient" — D5 (:179-192) is entirely about `$HOME` vs `tool_home_dir`; it defines no per-class check.
ADR-021:326-329 (Sharp edge 2): "it must be added as an explicit typed carve-out (D5's per-class check) rather than working by default."
ADR-017:376-380: "all `MC_HOME` attachment, output, workflow, ... and runtime-auth roots, including the allowlist, **except each exact typed own-source grant in Decisions 4, 6, and 8**".
ADR-017:397-399: "the exact own session, derived own state, selected runtime-control directory, or generated projection may be inside `MC_HOME`, but any sibling/ancestor/other identity is still denied."
ADR-021:118-119 (D3): "**the whole `MC_HOME` tree is one protected root**".
Probe (`mc/boundary`, prototype of D1's exact signature + D3's whole-tree root + D4's bidirectional `os.SameFile` walk, compiled and run with `mise exec -- go test ./boundary/ -run TestProbe_TypedOwnSession -v`):
  Rejects(own session   .../001/sessions/sess-own)   = source ".../sessions/sess-own" is under protected root MC_HOME(whole)
  Rejects(other session .../001/sessions/sess-other) = source ".../sessions/sess-other" is under protected root MC_HOME(whole)
  RESULT: own typed grant REJECTED
D7 solves own/other discrimination only for Worksources — ADR-021:252-257 injects "the own Worksource's identity explicitly" — and says nothing about own vs other typed MC_HOME grants.
ADR-021's only typed test (:370-371) — "`Rejects` is callable with no allowlist and still denies a sibling/ancestor/other identity inside `MC_HOME`" — tests only the deny half and PASSES on the probe above, so the test list would not catch this.
raised.failure_scenario: An implementer following ADR-021 builds `Rejects(id SourceIdentity) error` with a whole-tree MC_HOME root (D3) and bidirectional identity comparison (D4), and wires it into the typed-system spawn path as D1 rationale #1 and ADR-017:349-351 require. Every typed grant inside MC_HOME — own session at `MC_HOME/sessions/<id>` (ADR-017:663), derived own state, the selected `runtime_control_dir`, generated projections — is rejected with `mount.denied_root`. No agent can spawn at all. The listed typed-source test still passes, so the failure is discovered only at Phase 3 integration. The second implementer, noticing the ADR references a "per-class check" that does not exist, invents one — with a different signature, a different notion of "the class it claims", and different placement — because the ADR names no seam for it. Additionally, ADR-017:371-373's four permitted `.mission-control` shapes ("only the exact own task-local root, committed-tree materializations, trusted setup/landing, and Homie's type-matched inert nested covers"), flagged as a missed requirement in the extract §7.1, are dropped entirely: ADR-021 D7:233-235 lists "`.mission-control` roots" as a flat `denied_root` member with no carve-out, so a Worker's own task-local root rejects too.

raised.title: D1 and D2/D8 contradict each other on what `JurisdictionInput` carries, leaving the ADR's central seam undefined and the 512-before-stat rule and D8's algorithm without a home
raised.claim: D1 states `JurisdictionInput` carries already-canonical identities resolved by the host caller; D2 states `ResolveJurisdiction` takes `denied_paths` and checks the 512 bound "before any `os.Stat` or identity walk"; D8 requires resolving absent members through their nearest existing canonical ancestor. These cannot all be true. If the input is pre-resolved `(device,inode,type)` triples, `ResolveJurisdiction` performs no `os.Stat` at all — D2's "before any `os.Stat`" is vacuous inside the package, the pinning test "512+1 deny paths rejects before any stat" has nothing to observe, and D8's nearest-existing-ancestor resolution and "ambiguity denies" happen host-side in code ADR-021 explicitly does not govern. If instead `ResolveJurisdiction` takes paths and stats them (as `ResolveAllowlist` already does via `ResolveSource`), D1's flat statement about the input type is false. The ADR never says which, and the type is its principal contribution.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021:76-79 (D1): "It keeps `mc/boundary` pure. `JurisdictionInput` carries **already-canonical identities** (`device`, `inode`, `type`), resolved by the caller — the host side — from canonical spine records."
ADR-021:97-102 (D2): "`ResolveJurisdiction` is the **only** constructor. It takes the operator-derived members (`denied_paths`) and the MC-derived ones (MC_HOME roots, HOME-class roots, gateway/CA, sessions, other Worksources' roots, Git control dirs) and returns a value..."
ADR-021:104-106 (D2): "The 512-`denied_paths` bound (ADR-017:167-169) is checked **before any `os.Stat` or identity walk** and rejects rather than truncates".
ADR-021:268-271 (D8): "**`denied_paths` and any absent protected root**: resolve the nearest existing canonical ancestor, retain the unresolved suffix, and compare on that pair. **Ambiguity denies**" — an operation only performable by something that touches the filesystem.
ADR-021:372-373 (tests): "**Absence** (D8): a non-existent deny path resolves through its nearest existing ancestor; ambiguity denies" and :356-357: "512+1 deny paths rejects before any stat" — both listed as fast-lane `mc/boundary` tests, which requires the package to do the resolving.
The existing package does stat (`identity.go:140` `os.Stat(canonical)`, `identity.go:204` `os.Stat(parent)`, `identity.go:289` `os.Stat(current)`), so "pure" here plainly does not mean "no filesystem" — which is why D1's "already-canonical, resolved by the caller" reads as a deliberate and conflicting choice rather than a loose phrase.
D1:80-82 pins the ambiguity in place by grounding the pre-resolved reading in `phase3-contract.md:41` ("this contract does not pretend the warm helper can see unmounted host Worksource paths"), which argues for host-side resolution of *other Worksources' roots* specifically — but D2 folds `denied_paths` into the same constructor without distinguishing them.
raised.failure_scenario: Implementer A reads D1 and defines `type JurisdictionInput struct { DeniedPaths []ResolvedIdentity; ... }`, does the stat'ing in the host planner, and drops D2's bound check and D8's ancestor rule from `mc/boundary` because there is nothing to check there — the 512 bound then lives in un-ADR'd host code and `mc dispatch`'s two sides no longer share it, breaking `phase3-contract.md:169`'s "same pure policy package" requirement that D1 rationale #3 invokes. Implementer B reads D2/D8 and defines `type JurisdictionInput struct { DeniedPaths []string; ... }` with `ResolveJurisdiction` stat'ing every member. The two are wire-incompatible, and the profile-save and spawn call sites D9 names (:296) get different verdicts on the same absent deny path.

raised.title: D3 — the ADR's most consequential deviation — has no pinning test and no planted mutant; deleting the whole-tree MC_HOME root leaves every listed test green
raised.claim: D3 decides that the whole MC_HOME tree is one protected root, on the explicit rationale that an enumeration "silently drifts from the on-disk layout — the day someone adds `MC_HOME/quarantine/`, an enumerated-only rule permits it and no test fails." ADR-021's "Tests that pin it" section reproduces exactly that hole: it pins the fifteen enumerated classes and a sibling inside MC_HOME, but never an unenumerated MC_HOME child, and the planted-mutant list omits "whole-tree MC_HOME root removed." The mutant D3's own rationale describes therefore survives the test suite D3 ships with.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021:125-129 (D3): "An enumeration silently drifts from the on-disk layout — the day someone adds `MC_HOME/quarantine/`, an enumerated-only rule permits it and no test fails. A whole-tree rule cannot drift."
ADR-021:350-353 (tests, Union membership): "every one of the fifteen `MC_HOME` classes, sessions, the `.mission-control` and Git-control roots, gateway/CA private-key roots, every runtime-control dir (selected *and* other), each HOME-class root when present and its absence when not, and profile `denied_paths` — each rejects when allowlisted" — the fifteen classes, never MC_HOME itself, never an unenumerated child.
ADR-021:376-379 (planted mutants): "identity→prefix comparison; ancestor direction removed; `broad_root` as a literal `/Users` list; jurisdiction after `ResolveAccess`; `denied_paths` made subtractable." — D3's mutant is absent.
D4's bidirectionality masks it in fixtures: ADR-021:132-133 concedes "bidirectionality (D4) already makes `MC_HOME` reject as an ancestor of `sessions/` — *but only if `sessions/` exists*", and every listed fixture creates `sessions/`.
Probe (`mise exec -- go test ./boundary/ -run TestProbe_D3Mutant -v`), enumeration-only Jurisdiction, exercising every test named in ADR-021:350-353 and :370-371:
  all fifteen classes reject — mutant not killed
  sibling `MC_HOME/sessions/other` rejects — mutant not killed
  Rejects(MC_HOME/quarantine) under mutant = <nil>
  MUTANT SURVIVES every test named in ADR-021's 'Tests that pin it'; MC_HOME/quarantine is mountable.
D1's other undertested law: "a zero `Jurisdiction` passed to `Authorize` is a programming error that fails closed (rejects everything)" (:86-88) has no test in :347-379 and no named mutant either.
raised.failure_scenario: The slice is built TDD-first from the listed tests. An implementer who finds the whole-tree root redundant (D4 already rejects MC_HOME as an ancestor of `sessions/` in every fixture) removes it as a simplification — precisely the reader Sharp edge 1 warns about, applied to D3 instead of D6. The full suite stays green. A later `MC_HOME/quarantine/` or any new MC_HOME class is then mountable as an ordinary profile mount, silently violating ADR-017:345 ("complete `MC_HOME`" absent) and `phase3-contract.md:178` ("broad `MC_HOME`" absent from in-container probes) — the exact drift D3 exists to prevent, with no red test to announce it. The zero-value law fails the same way: a `Jurisdiction` built as a plain struct with no validity discriminator passes every listed test while permitting everything on the zero value.

raised.title: D4's unqualified ancestor rule rejects the own Worksource's own workspace_root, which ADR-017:302-303 requires to authorize
raised.claim: D4 states the ancestor direction as universal over every protected root, with no carve-out for the nested-cover mechanism ADR-017 Decision 6 uses. Because ADR-017:370-371 makes `<workspace_root>/.mission-control` task/projection roots a protected union member in their own right, and ADR-017:718-720 has ONBOARDING create them, every onboarded Worksource's workspace_root becomes an ancestor of a protected root and is rejected with mount.denied_root — while ADR-017:302-303 requires that exact source to pass Authorize as an ordinary allowlisted mount. Implemented as written, no onboarded Worksource can ever be mounted.
raised.evidence: ADR-021:144-147 states the rule with no exception: "For canonical source `S` and protected root `P`, reject when `S == P`, `S` is a descendant of `P`, **or `S` is an ancestor of `P`**." ADR-017:370-371 lists as a union member "every registered real Git control directory and every `<workspace_root>/.mission-control` task/projection root". ADR-017:718-720: "Onboarding creates the operator-owned non-symlink `.mission-control/{tasks,projections}` roots". ADR-017:302-303: "Every externally configured `workspace_root`, `artifact_roots`, and `readonly_mounts` source must match one allow entry." ADR-017's actual answer for this nested protected root is a COVER, not a rejection — Decision 6:639: "`/workspace/source/.mission-control` | generated empty directory, RO cover over the reserved repository-root path", and :658 the same for the Homie workspace mount. ADR-021 never mentions covers: `grep -n "cover\|workspace_root\|own Worksource" docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md` returns only lines 209, 213, 384 — all unrelated prose uses of the word "cover". PROBE (mc/boundary/jurisdiction.go + probe_test.go in worktree, D1's signatures verbatim, fixture = onboarding's exact layout): `--- FAIL: TestProbeOwnWorkspaceRootIsAncestorOfItsOwnMissionControl` / `Rejects(own workspace_root ".../home/src/acme") -> source ".../home/src/acme" is an ancestor of protected denied_path ".../home/src/acme/.mission-control/tasks"` / `ADR-017:302-303 requires the own workspace_root to authorize; ADR-021 D4 rejected it with "mount.denied_root"`. D3's whole-MC_HOME typed carve-out does not reach this: the workspace_root is an ordinary profile-requested mount, not a typed system source.
raised.failure_scenario: An implementer follows D4 literally and writes the mirror walk over each protected root's ancestors. Operator onboards Worksource `~/src/acme` (ADR-017 Decision 1's own example) and allowlists it `access = "rw"`. Onboarding creates `~/src/acme/.mission-control/{tasks,projections}`. Profile save calls Authorize("~/src/acme", RW, blocked, j) -> mount.denied_root. Per ADR-021 D9 / ADR-017:1165 that aborts the whole plan. Every Worksource is unmountable from the moment it is onboarded; the product cannot spawn a single pipeline container. The bug is invisible in ADR-021's own test list, which never constructs a workspace_root containing its own `.mission-control` root.

raised.title: D1's Rejects(id SourceIdentity) signature cannot express the typed carve-out ADR-017:396-401 mandates, and the mechanism D3 and Sharp edge 2 both delegate to "D5" does not exist in D5
raised.claim: ADR-017:396-401 requires jurisdiction to PERMIT a typed source's exact own root inside MC_HOME while DENYING any sibling/ancestor/other identity. D1's predicate takes only a SourceIdentity — no kind, no claimed class — so own-session and sibling-session present identically and must receive identical verdicts. D3 and Sharp edge 2 each delegate the missing discrimination to "D5's per-class check", but D5 is titled "HOME is injected, not ambient" and contains no per-class check. The carve-out mechanism is cited twice and specified nowhere.
raised.evidence: ADR-021:58: `func (j Jurisdiction) Rejects(id SourceIdentity) error` — the only parameter is the identity. ADR-021:71-73 makes the call mandatory for typed sources: "A jurisdiction that lives on the allowlist is unreachable for the sources that most need it. `Rejects` is therefore exported and total on its own." ADR-017:396-400 is the requirement: "A typed system mount is instead confined to its one kind-specific authorized root: the exact own session, derived own state, selected runtime-control directory, or generated projection **may be inside `MC_HOME`**, but any sibling/ancestor/other identity is still denied." ADR-017:319 grants "own run/session folder: RW into its owner only"; Decision 6:663 mounts it at `/mc/session`. The two dangling cross-references: ADR-021:136-138 "The enumeration is kept anyway, because it is not redundant in the **descendant** direction for the typed-grant carve-out (D5): a typed source must be checked against the exact class it claims"; ADR-021:328 "it must be added as an explicit typed carve-out (D5's per-class check)". D5's entire body (ADR-021:179-192) is about injecting HOME instead of reading `$HOME` — verified by direct read; it contains no class, kind, or carve-out concept. ADR-021's test list only ever pins the deny half — :370-371: "**Typed sources** (D1): `Rejects` is callable with no allowlist and still denies a sibling/ancestor/other identity inside `MC_HOME` (:396-401)." The permit half ADR-017 requires in the same sentence is never tested. PROBE output (D1's signature verbatim, D3's whole-tree MC_HOME): `Rejects(own session (ADR-017:396-399 REQUIRES permit)) -> source ".../sessions/0123456789abcdef" is at or under protected MC_HOME ".../.mission-control-dev"` and `Rejects(sibling session (ADR-017:399 requires deny)) -> source ".../sessions/fedcba9876543210" is at or under protected MC_HOME`. Identical verdicts; the predicate is blind to the distinction ADR-017 turns on.
raised.failure_scenario: An implementer builds D1's signature and D3's whole-tree MC_HOME root, and writes the test ADR-021:370-371 specifies — sibling inside MC_HOME denied. It passes. Every typed system source is then also denied: the own session folder (`/mc/session`), derived own state (`/home/agent`), the four package caches, the selected runtime-control dir (`/home/agent/.codex`), the seal root, the attachment pair. Every agent spawn aborts (ADR-017:1165). The implementer reaches for the carve-out D3 promised, opens D5, and finds a paragraph about `$HOME`. Alternatively, they resolve the ambiguity the other way — callers skip Rejects for typed sources — and then nothing enforces ADR-017:399's sibling denial, so a typed source can name another session's folder. The signature admits no third option: both readings violate ADR-017, and only a class/kind parameter Rejects does not have can satisfy both halves of :396-401.

raised.title: "Compile-time discoveries, zero risk" is false: D1's zero-value rule turns 7 of the 13 existing Authorize tests red at runtime
raised.claim: ADR-021 characterizes the fourth-parameter migration as compile-time only and zero risk. D1's own zero-value fail-closed rule makes that untrue: once call sites compile, every existing test that expects an ACCEPT fails at runtime, because the natural migration (pass the zero value) now rejects everything. Each of the 13 sites needs a real on-disk HOME + MC_HOME fixture and a ResolveJurisdiction call — not a mechanical signature edit.
raised.evidence: ADR-021:383-384: "**`Authorize` grows a fourth parameter**, touching every existing call site and test in `mc/boundary`. Compile-time discoveries, zero risk." This collides with ADR-021:84-88: "a zero `Jurisdiction` passed to `Authorize` is a programming error that fails closed (rejects everything)". PROBE: built D1's seam, inserted `j.Rejects(identity)` at D6's position in identity.go, and applied the mechanical migration (`sed` the 13 sites to pass `boundary.Jurisdiction{}`). `mise exec -- go build ./...` is clean — Authorize has no production call site, so nothing outside tests breaks. `mise exec -- go test ./boundary/` then yields 7 runtime failures across 3 test funcs: `TestAuthorizeIdentityWalk/descendant_accepted_with_derived_suffix: unresolved jurisdiction rejects every source`; `.../the_root_itself_has_an_empty_suffix`; `.../in-root_symlink_accepted`; `.../RW_over_an_RO_maximum_rejects: code = "mount.denied_root", want "mount.rw_not_permitted"`; `TestAuthorizeBlockedMatching/a_workspace_merely_containing_a_blocked_name_is_fine`; `TestAuthorizeSuffixGrammar/colon_in_the_allow_root_itself_is_legal`; `.../ordinary_descendant_of_a_colon_root_is_legal`. Note the zero-value MECHANISM itself works — an unexported `resolved bool` set only by ResolveJurisdiction reproduces blocked.go:72-76's law without compiling members in, since unexported fields bar any outside package from minting a non-zero value. D1's zero-value claim is implementable; only its cost estimate is wrong.
raised.failure_scenario: A session plans the slice against ADR-021's "zero risk" line, budgets a sed-and-compile migration, and discovers mid-slice that the fast suite is red in 7 places and that each fixture must now materialize a HOME and MC_HOME directory tree and thread a constructed Jurisdiction through. Per AGENTS.md §4 the session may not commit while red; per §8 a mid-red quota death is the worst handoff — and this is precisely the estimate error that produces one.

raised.title: D1's jurisdiction seam has no kind parameter, so every typed MC_HOME grant ADR-017 Decision 6 mandates rejects; the "per-class check" the ADR twice defers to does not exist
raised.claim: ADR-021 mandates that typed system sources call `Jurisdiction.Rejects`, defines that predicate as the ADR-017:366-386 union, and gives it no kind/class input — so it cannot implement ADR-017:396-401's carve-out. An implementer following ADR-021 ships a boundary that rejects /mc/session, /home/agent, /mc/records/output, /mc/private/completion-seal, /mc/attachments/in and /workspace/operator/traces. This contradicts ADR-017, which wins.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021 D1 (021:57-59) defines the whole seam: `// Callable WITHOUT an allow-root match — typed system sources need exactly this.` / `func (j Jurisdiction) Rejects(id SourceIdentity) error` — one parameter, an identity, no kind. D1's rationale 1 (021:67-73) mandates the call: "**Typed system sources bypass `match()` entirely** but still owe the check … `Rejects` is therefore exported and total on its own."

But ADR-017:396-401 says the union does NOT govern typed mounts: "That union governs ordinary/profile-requested mounts. A typed system mount is **instead** confined to its one kind-specific authorized root: the exact own session, derived own state, selected runtime-control directory, or generated projection **may be inside `MC_HOME`**, but any sibling/ancestor/other identity is still denied." ADR-017:349-351 likewise requires only "its **kind-specific** source-type, non-symlink, identity, containment, and cross-Worksource checks".

ADR-021 twice points at a per-class mechanism that it does not contain. 021:137: "the typed-grant carve-out (D5): a typed source must be checked against the exact class it claims". 021:328: "it must be added as an explicit typed carve-out (D5's per-class check)". ADR-021's D5 (021:179-192) is titled "HOME is injected, not ambient" and is entirely about `JurisdictionInput` carrying HOME — `grep -n "D5" docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md` returns exactly lines 137, 179, 328. No per-class check is specified anywhere in ADR-021.

PROBE (mc/boundary/adr021_probe_internal_test.go — D1's signature, D3's whole-tree root, D4's bidirectional os.SameFile rule, implemented verbatim on the real `enclosesByIdentity`/`ResolveSource`):
```
$ cd mc && mise exec -- go test ./boundary/ -run TestProbe_ADR021_TypedGrantsInsideMCHome -v
REJECT  /mc/session (ADR-017:663, RW, every owner)         -> source ".../sessions/run-0123456789abcdef" is under protected root ".../001"
REJECT  /home/agent derived own state (ADR-017:681, RW)    -> source ".../state/worksources/ws-1/home" is under protected root ".../001"
REJECT  /mc/records/output (ADR-017:669, RW)               -> ... is under protected root ".../001"
REJECT  /mc/workflow/plan.js (ADR-017:675, RW)             -> ... is under protected root ".../001"
REJECT  /mc/private/completion-seal (ADR-017:666, RW)      -> ... is under protected root ".../001"
REJECT  /mc/attachments/in (ADR-017:664, RO)               -> ... is under protected root ".../001"
REJECT  /workspace/operator/traces (ADR-017:660, RO)       -> ... is under protected root ".../001"
```
All seven typed grants reject. CONTROL PROBE (adr021_ctrl_internal_test.go) isolates the cause — with D3's whole-tree root removed and only the enumerated `MC_HOME/sessions` root registered:
```
$ mise exec -- go test ./boundary/ -run TestProbe_ADR021_EnumerationOnly -v
enumeration-only STILL REJECTS own session: source ".../sessions/run-0123456789abcdef" is under protected root ".../sessions"
```
So the defect is D1's kindless seam (ADR-017:375-376 makes "all `MC_HOME/sessions`" a member and D4 rejects descendants); D3 only widens the blast radius from the fifteen enumerated classes to the entire tree. ADR-021's own test list never pins the permit side — 021:370-371 asks only that `Rejects` "still denies a sibling/ancestor/other identity inside `MC_HOME`".
raised.failure_scenario: An implementer follows ADR-021 as written and TDDs `func (j Jurisdiction) Rejects(id SourceIdentity) error`. Per D1 rationale 1 the resident calls it for typed system sources at plan and spawn. The own session source `MC_HOME/sessions/<run-id>` is a descendant of the protected root `MC_HOME/sessions` (and, per D3, of `MC_HOME`), so `Rejects` returns `mount.denied_root`. Per D9/ADR-017:1165 every code aborts the whole plan. Result: `/mc/session` — which ADR-017:663 grants RW to every owner and which spec §15.4's resume substrate depends on — can never be planned, and no agent can launch. The same abort fires for `/home/agent` (:681), `/mc/records/output` (:669), the completion seal (:666) and the trace projection (:660). ADR-021 ships a boundary whose test suite is green (it only pins the deny side) and whose production planner cannot spawn any container.

raised.title: D3's cite :376-380 truncates the exception clause at :380-381, and its "adds no member ADR-017 does not already imply" is false
raised.claim: ADR-021 D3 justifies the whole-tree MC_HOME root by claiming it adds nothing ADR-017 does not already imply, citing ADR-017:376-380 as "fifteen root classes". The actual bullet is :377-381, and the line D3's range stops one short of carries the exception clause that refutes the claim.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021 D3 (021:116-119): "ADR-017:376-380 enumerates fifteen root classes"; and 021:130-131: "**Deviates least.** It adds no member ADR-017 does not already imply: every enumerated class is inside the tree".

The real ADR-017 text (`awk 'NR>=374 && NR<=382' docs/adr/017-mount-authorization.md`):
```
376:   separate typed projection, never a session source as an ordinary mount;
377: - all `MC_HOME` attachment, output, workflow, correction, revision, context,
378:   projection, seal, landing, state, cache, config, control, backup, and
379:   runtime-auth roots,
380:   including the allowlist, except each exact typed own-source grant in
381:   Decisions 4, 6, and 8;
```
Line 376 is the tail of the *sessions* bullet, not the enumeration; the enumeration runs :377-381. The cited range :376-380 ends exactly one line before `Decisions 4, 6, and 8;` completes the clause "**except each exact typed own-source grant in** Decisions 4, 6, and 8". ADR-017's enumeration is therefore not a bare list of fifteen roots — it is fifteen roots *minus every typed own-source grant*. D3's whole-tree root carries no such exception, so it does add members ADR-017 excludes (every row of Decision 6 that lives under MC_HOME). ADR-021's own Sharp Edge 2 (021:325-326) concedes "D3's whole-tree `MC_HOME` protection is stricter than ADR-017's enumeration", which contradicts the ADR's headline claim at 021:9-10: "It **adds nothing to ADR-017's policy** — every rule below is ADR-017's".
raised.failure_scenario: A reviewer or implementer checks D3's "deviates least" argument against the cited :376-380, sees a clean fifteen-class list with no exception, and accepts the whole-tree root as equivalent. The dropped :380-381 exception clause is the only thing in Decision 5 that authorizes typed own-source grants inside MC_HOME, so its loss is what makes the finding above total rather than partial. Per AGENTS.md §6, a resolution that silently drops an accepted ADR's exception clause is not "deviates least from the spec's text".

raised.title: D7's central cite :368-369 for `mount.cross_worksource` points at the `denied_paths` bullet, which D7 assigns to the other code
raised.claim: ADR-021 D7 defines `mount.cross_worksource` as "the ADR-017:368-369 member only". ADR-017:368 is the `denied_paths` bullet, which D7 itself routes to `mount.denied_root`. The other-Worksource member is :369-370. The cite is not merely off by one — it half-covers the wrong member, and D7 uses the same wrong range a second time.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021 D7 (021:230-232): "- **`mount.cross_worksource`** — the ADR-017:368-369 member only: another Worksource's workspace / worktree / artifact / state / cache / tool-home root." And 021:239-240 repeats it: "Decision 5 puts other-Worksource roots *inside* the one union". D4 also anchors on it implicitly via D7's split.

The real ADR-017 text (`awk 'NR>=366 && NR<=370' docs/adr/017-mount-authorization.md`):
```
366: The non-subtractable protected set is the union of:
367:
368: - the selected effective profile's `denied_paths` entries;
369: - every other Worksource's workspace/worktree/artifact/state/cache/tool-home
370:   roots;
```
Line 368 is `denied_paths`. D7's own next bullet (021:233-236) assigns `denied_paths` to the opposite code: "- **`mount.denied_root`** — every other member: profile `denied_paths`, Git control dirs …". So D7's two bullets, read against the real line numbers, both claim :368. The requirements extract ADR-021 is built on carries the same drift (`docs/reviews/2026-07-14-adr-017-jurisdiction-extract.md:23` "**Every *other* Worksource's roots** (:368-369)"), so the error is inherited rather than independently checked — which is precisely what the extract exists to prevent.
raised.failure_scenario: The TDD implementer builds the D7 split from the cite rather than the quoted prose, resolves :368-369 in ADR-017, and finds `denied_paths` at :368. Either they wire `denied_paths` into `mount.cross_worksource` — inverting D7's split and breaking the acceptance row at ADR-017:1174 ("own/other/parent Worksource") plus D7's own precedence test ("a path that is both reports `cross_worksource`", 021:366-367) — or they stall on a cite that contradicts the sentence containing it. D7's precedence rule, which exists specifically to make the both-member case deterministic, is defined against a range where the two members are not the two members it names.

raised.title: Acceptance-row cites in D4 and D6 are each off by one, and D6's is attached to the wrong row
raised.claim: ADR-021 cites ADR-017:1173 for "protected ancestors" (actually :1172) and ADR-017:1174 for "real-HOME broad root vs an allowed descendant" (actually :1173). Both are load-bearing: they are the acceptance rows D4 and D6 name as the justification for their contested decisions.
raised.evidence:
`grep -n` on the real ADR-017:
```
1172:ordinary workspace containing one; protected ancestors; all four RO/RW
1173:cells; source kinds; real-HOME broad root vs an allowed descendant; selected
1174:vs other runtime-control dirs; own/other/parent Worksource; and missing or
```
ADR-021 D4 (021:158-160): "ADR-017:1173 names the class (*\"protected ancestors\"*) and :1174 pairs it with *\"own/other/parent Worksource\"*" — "protected ancestors" is at :1172; only the second half is right.
ADR-021 D4 again (021:176-177): "It is the only fit, and :1174 files the case beside the other jurisdiction cases" — the broad_root case is at :1173.
ADR-021 D6 (021:216-217): "This makes :1174's *\"real-HOME broad root vs an **allowed** descendant\"* pairing read as deliberate rather than incidental" — that phrase is at :1173, not :1174.
Two further drifts of the same kind: D9 (021:296) cites "profile save (:237-238, spec:453)" but `grep -n "Profile save and every spawn"` returns `239:`; :237 is the `## Decision 3` heading. D1 (021:81-82) cites the warning "*\"warm helper cannot see unmounted host Worksource paths\"* (contract:41)" but `awk 'NR>=46 && NR<=47' docs/phase3-contract.md` shows it at :46-47; contract:41-44 is the separate purity rule D1 also cites correctly. Each drift is inherited verbatim from `docs/reviews/2026-07-14-adr-017-jurisdiction-extract.md` (§3 "(:1173 *\"protected ancestors\"*, :1174 …)", §7.7 "(:237-238…)", §5 "Contract:41 also warns").
raised.failure_scenario: The reviewer or implementer opens ADR-017 at :1173 to confirm D4's claim that the ADR itself names "protected ancestors" as an acceptance class, and reads "cells; source kinds; real-HOME broad root…" instead. Since D4's whole case for the load-bearing ancestor direction and D4's case for borrowing `mount.denied_root` for `broad_root` both rest on these two rows, an unverifiable cite either stalls the slice or gets accepted on trust. The systematic pattern — every drift matches the extract, none matches the source — shows the cites were copied from the extract rather than rechecked, so the extract's line numbers cannot be relied on as ADR-021's "factual base" (021:32-34).

raised.title: D1's injected (device,inode,type) identities cannot be compared by D4's mandated os.SameFile — the composition fails OPEN
raised.claim: ADR-021 D1 requires JurisdictionInput to carry protected-root identities as caller-resolved `(device, inode, type)` triples, while D4 requires that *every* jurisdiction comparison be `os.SameFile`. These two decisions are mutually unsatisfiable: `os.SameFile` type-asserts on the unexported concrete type `*os.fileStat` and returns `false` for any FileInfo the caller synthesizes from a triple — even when the dev/ino are correct. `false` means "not the same file", i.e. no rejection. An implementer following both decisions literally ships a jurisdiction check that authorizes every protected root, silently.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021 D1, `docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md:76-79`: "It keeps `mc/boundary` pure. `JurisdictionInput` carries **already-canonical identities** (`device`, `inode`, `type`), resolved by the caller — the host side — from canonical spine records."

ADR-021 D4, `docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md:144-147`: "For canonical source `S` and protected root `P`, reject when `S == P`, `S` is a descendant of `P`, **or `S` is an ancestor of `P`**. Every comparison is `os.SameFile` on resolved objects — never a string prefix".

Go stdlib (`$GOROOT/src/os/types.go`), read via `mise exec -- go env GOROOT`:
```go
func SameFile(fi1, fi2 FileInfo) bool {
	fs1, ok1 := fi1.(*fileStat)
	fs2, ok2 := fi2.(*fileStat)
	if !ok1 || !ok2 {
		return false
	}
	return sameFile(fs1, fs2)
}
```
The assertion is on the **unexported concrete type**; `Sys()` is never consulted. Only a live `os.Stat`/`os.Lstat` result can ever be an argument.

PROBE (written into the real package as `mc/boundary/adr021_probe_test.go`, implementing D1's triple + D4's SameFile walk in the `enclosesByIdentity` shape at `mc/boundary/identity.go:198-210`; run with `mise exec -- go test ./boundary/ -run TestADR021_D1 -v`, since removed):
```
=== RUN   TestADR021_D1_triples_defeat_D4_SameFile
    adr021_probe_test.go:86: injected protected identity: dev=16777231 ino=65204688 (correct)
    adr021_probe_test.go:91: ESCAPE: source ".../mc-home/sessions/run-0123456789abcdef" is a
        descendant of protected root ".../mc-home/sessions", the injected (dev,ino) is CORRECT,
        yet D4's os.SameFile walk never matched. Jurisdiction fails OPEN.
    adr021_probe_test.go:109: control (real os.Stat FileInfo on both sides) rejects = true
--- FAIL: TestADR021_D1_triples_defeat_D4_SameFile (0.00s)
```
A second probe confirmed the failure survives a correctly-populated `*syscall.Stat_t` behind `Sys()`:
```
A. os.SameFile(real, synth-with-Stat_t)   = false
B. os.SameFile(real, synth-without-Stat_t) = false   <-- fails OPEN
C. os.SameFile(real, real2)                = true
```
D1 cites the existing `BlockPolicy` as its model, but `BlockPolicy` compares *strings*; it never had to round-trip a filesystem identity through a struct. The existing `os.SameFile` call sites (`identity.go:185`, `:205`, `:291`) all compare two live `os.Stat` results, which is why the seam has not bitten yet.
raised.failure_scenario: Host side resolves protected root `MC_HOME/sessions` to `(dev=16777231, ino=65204688, dir)` and injects it in `JurisdictionInput`. A profile allowlists `MC_HOME` and requests `MC_HOME/sessions/run-<other>`. `j.Rejects(id)` walks the source's ancestor chain calling `os.SameFile(liveInfo, injectedInfo)`; every call returns `false` because `injectedInfo` is not an `*os.fileStat`. `Rejects` returns nil, `Authorize` returns an Authorization, and another run's session bytes are mounted. No test in ADR-021's own "Tests that pin it" list catches this: they all construct protected roots from real paths, so a test-only helper that stats the path would pass while production injection fails open. The design must either state that `JurisdictionInput` carries live `fs.FileInfo` from a host-side `os.Stat` (dropping D1's triple), or drop D4's `os.SameFile` mandate in favour of an explicit dev/ino equality predicate — and then say so, because "never a string prefix" was the whole point.

raised.title: D3's whole-MC_HOME rule has no carve-out mechanism: `Rejects(id SourceIdentity)` cannot distinguish a typed source's own grant from a sibling's, and the cited "D5" carve-out does not exist
raised.claim: D3 protects the whole MC_HOME tree and defers the typed-grant carve-out to "D5's per-class check" — but ADR-021's D5 is "HOME is injected, not ambient" and contains no carve-out. No decision in ADR-021 specifies the mechanism, and D1's signature `func (j Jurisdiction) Rejects(id SourceIdentity) error` admits none: `SourceIdentity` carries only Raw/RawClean/Canonical/Info/IsDir, so the predicate cannot know which class a typed source claims or which run owns it. ADR-017:396-401 requires exactly that discrimination. The design is therefore unimplementable as written, and both available repairs are wrong.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021 D3, `docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md:136-138`: "The enumeration is kept anyway, because it is not redundant in the **descendant** direction for the typed-grant carve-out (D5): a typed source must be checked against the exact class it claims, not merely \"inside `MC_HOME`\"."

ADR-021 Sharp edge 2, `:327-329`: "it must be added as an explicit typed carve-out (D5's per-class check) rather than working by default."

But ADR-021 D5, `:179-184` — the entirety of the decision those two lines point at:
> "### D5. HOME is injected, not ambient / **Resolves gap (e).** / `JurisdictionInput` carries the operator's real HOME as an **explicit, already-resolved** value. It is never read from `$HOME` inside `mc/boundary`."

`grep -n "D5" docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md` returns exactly three hits — `:137`, `:179`, `:328` — so there is no third place where a per-class check is defined.

The signature that must carry the class, `docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md:58`:
```go
func (j Jurisdiction) Rejects(id SourceIdentity) error
```
and the type it receives, `mc/boundary/identity.go:110-116`: `SourceIdentity{Raw, RawClean, Canonical string; Info fs.FileInfo; IsDir bool}` — no class, no owner, no run id.

What ADR-017 requires of that predicate, `docs/adr/017-mount-authorization.md:396-401`: "A typed system mount is instead confined to its one kind-specific authorized root: the exact own session, derived own state, selected runtime-control directory, or generated projection may be inside `MC_HOME`, but any sibling/ancestor/other identity is still denied." And `:376-381`: the MC_HOME roots are protected "except each exact typed own-source grant in Decisions 4, 6, and 8."

The grants D3 would kill, from ADR-017 Decision 6 (`grep -n "MC_HOME/" docs/adr/017-mount-authorization.md`): `:663` `/mc/session` ← `MC_HOME/sessions/<run-or-session-id>/`; `:664` `/mc/attachments/in`; `:666` `/mc/private/completion-seal` ← `MC_HOME/seals/<run-id>/`; `:668` `/mc/private/attachments/out`; `:669` `/mc/records/output` ← `MC_HOME/outputs/<run-id>/`; `:675` `/mc/workflow/plan.js`; `:681` `/home/agent` ← `MC_HOME/state/worksources/<scope-id>/home`.

ADR-021's own test list, `:370-371`, tests only the denial arm: "**Typed sources** (D1): `Rejects` is callable with no allowlist and still denies a sibling/ancestor/other identity inside `MC_HOME` (:396-401)." A `Rejects` that denies *everything* inside MC_HOME passes this test.
raised.failure_scenario: Two mutually exclusive failures, and the implementer must pick one. (a) Follow D3 literally: `j.Rejects(id)` denies every identity inside the MC_HOME tree, so the Worker's own `/mc/session`, `/home/agent`, and `/mc/records/output` all reject at plan time and no agent container can be planned at all — Phase 3's "Complete mount plan" row (`docs/phase3-contract.md:178`) can never go green, and ADR-021's typed-source test still passes, hiding it. (b) Add the bypass the missing carve-out implies — "typed sources skip MC_HOME jurisdiction" — and because `Rejects` receives no class to check the identity against, *any* typed source reaches *any* MC_HOME path: a run-output typed source naming `MC_HOME/mount-allowlist` (RW), or `MC_HOME/sessions/<other-run>`, or `MC_HOME/seals/<other-run>`. That is precisely ADR-017:400-401's "This is a closed exception, not a way for an allowlist entry to override `MC_HOME` protection", defeated. The repair requires `Rejects` to take the claimed grant (class + owning run/session identity) and validate the source identity against that exact class's authorized root — a parameter the ADR must add and does not have.

raised.title: D9 re-evaluates `Rejects` but never re-resolves `JurisdictionInput`, so ADR-017:1339-1341's "protected-set changes" arm is not delivered (TOCTOU on a replaced protected root)
raised.claim: D9 claims to implement ADR-017:264-269 and :1339-1341, but it only forbids caching the *verdict* and mandates re-running `Rejects`. `Rejects` is by D9's own words "a pure function of `(identity, jurisdiction)`" — re-running it against an unchanged `Jurisdiction` value necessarily returns the same answer. For a protected-set change with an unchanged source inode to reject, `ResolveJurisdiction` must itself re-run (real `os.Stat` of the protected roots) at each of step 7's rechecks. ADR-021 nowhere requires that; Sharp edge 4 prices the build at "every spawn" (once), and affirmatively blesses a cache keyed on a "generation" that no mechanism in ADR-021 ties to any on-disk observation.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021 D9, `docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md:283-296`, header: "### D9. The verdict is never cached on source identity / **Implements ADR-017:264-269 and :1339-1341.**" and its operative sentence at `:292-296`: "So a **protected-set change alone, with the source inode unchanged, must reject**. That forbids memoizing a jurisdiction verdict keyed on source identity — the obvious optimization, and a wrong one. `Rejects` is a pure function of `(identity, jurisdiction)` and is re-evaluated at every call site: profile save (:237-238, spec:453), plan, pre-create, and post-create/pre-start."

Note what is re-evaluated: `Rejects`. `jurisdiction` is a *parameter*, supplied by the caller. D9 imposes no obligation on it.

ADR-021 Sharp edge 4, `:335-340`: "**`JurisdictionInput` is a wide value** ... Building it is host-side work with real `os.Stat` cost **at every spawn**, and D9 forbids caching it away. ... If it ever matters, the fix is caching keyed on the *jurisdiction's* own generation, never on source identity." — "at every spawn" is once per spawn; step 7 requires two rechecks *within* one spawn. And a "generation"-keyed cache is exactly a stale-identity cache unless the generation is bumped by a filesystem observation, which ADR-021 never specifies.

What ADR-017 actually requires, `docs/adr/017-mount-authorization.md:1339-1341` (verified via `sed -n '1330,1360p'`): "Permission/ACL, blocked-policy, allow-root, and **protected-set** changes with unchanged source bytes/inode also remove the unstarted container."

And `docs/adr/017-mount-authorization.md:264-269`: "Immediately before Docker create and again after create/before start, rerun **the whole predicate**: ... protected/cross-Worksource roots; kind, access, and final destination. Any drift removes the unstarted container. An unchanged inode/bytes does not excuse a changed owner, mode, or ACL." — "the whole predicate" includes resolving the protected roots, not merely re-comparing against a snapshot of them.
raised.failure_scenario: `ResolveJurisdiction` runs once at plan time and resolves another Worksource's workspace root `/Users/op/src/other-ws` to inode N. Between plan and pre-start, that root is replaced on the host (rmdir+recreate, or a rename swap — ordinary Worksource re-registration or repo re-clone does this), so the path now carries inode M. The candidate's allowlisted source resolves to inode M. Step 7's pre-create and pre-start rechecks dutifully re-run `Rejects(identity, jurisdiction)` — but `jurisdiction` still holds inode N, `os.SameFile` finds no match, and the verdict is nil both times. The container starts with another Worksource's workspace root mounted, which is exactly the drift :264-269 says "removes the unstarted container". D9 is the decision that claims to close this and, as written, does not. The fix is one sentence D9 is missing: `ResolveJurisdiction` re-runs at plan, pre-create, and post-create/pre-start, and a changed protected-root identity is itself drift.

raised.title: D5 relocates trust in HOME to the caller but specifies no validation; D1's fail-closed law covers only the zero value, so a wrong injected HOME silently deletes both broad_root and all 14 HOME-class roots
raised.claim: D5 argues at length that `$HOME` is untrustworthy, then requires the caller to supply it "already-resolved" and specifies no check on what arrives. D2 says `ResolveJurisdiction` is "the only constructor" and is "validated" but never says HOME is among the validated members. D1's fail-closed law is scoped to the *zero* value only. A `Jurisdiction` with a non-zero but wrong HOME is therefore constructible, and it fails open in two directions at once.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021 D5, `docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md:183-184`: "`JurisdictionInput` carries the operator's real HOME as an **explicit, already-resolved** value. It is never read from `$HOME` inside `mc/boundary`." — no absoluteness, non-root, existence, directory-kind, or ownership check is stated anywhere in D2 or D5.

ADR-021 D1's fail-closed law, `:84-87`: "`Jurisdiction`'s zero value must be **safe, not empty**: per D2 it cannot be constructed without its non-operator members, and a **zero** `Jurisdiction` passed to `Authorize` is a programming error that fails closed (rejects everything) rather than an empty set that permits everything." — the guarantee is about the zero value, not about a populated-but-wrong one.

The two rules that consume HOME, D4 `:161-165`: "`S == HOME` or `S` a strict ancestor of HOME rejects, while descendants stay eligible"; and ADR-017:383, the HOME-class roots are members only "**when present**", which ADR-021 D8 `:272-274` restates: "The HOME-class roots carry an explicit *\"when present\"* (:383), so an absent `~/.aws` is simply not a member — it cannot be intersected and it cannot be ambiguous."

Compare D5's own stated standard, `:188-189`: "A boundary that can be relocated by an environment variable is not a boundary." D5 relocates the boundary from an env var to an unvalidated struct field — the trust is moved, not established.
raised.failure_scenario: The host side injects `HOME = "/"` (an unset-HOME fallback, a service-account resolution, or a plain plumbing bug — the exact class of error D5's `ownerUID` precedent exists to prevent). Two protections vanish silently and simultaneously: (1) broad_root degenerates — `/` has no strict ancestors, so only `/` itself rejects, and per D4 "descendants stay eligible" makes every path on the machine broad_root-eligible; (2) the 14 HOME-class roots re-anchor to `/.ssh`, `/.aws`, `/Library/Keychains`, … which do not exist, so per D8's "when present" rule every one of them is "simply not a member". An `[[allow]]` entry for `/Users/op` then authorizes the operator's entire real HOME: the blocked floor matches nothing (`users`, `op` are no patterns, `mc/boundary/blocked.go:28-69`), and per AGENTS.md's environment facts MC_HOME is a scratch path outside HOME, so D3's whole-tree rule does not cover it either. `ResolveJurisdiction` returns no error and `Authorize` returns an Authorization. D2 should name HOME as a validated member: absolute, non-`/`, existing, a directory, owned by `ownerUID` — the same seam `TrustHomeDir` already applies to MC_HOME at `identity.go:68-77`.

raised.title: D4's identity-based containment does not settle hardlinks, and ADR-021 never notices — a hardlink to a file inside a protected directory is invisible to all three arms
raised.claim: D4 asserts that identity comparison is the complete answer to aliasing ("Every comparison is `os.SameFile` ... never a string prefix"), and its test list enumerates only symlink aliases. But identity-based *containment* compares path lineage, and a hardlink gives an inode a second lineage outside the protected root. `os.SameFile` catches a hardlink to a protected root *itself* (the `S == P` arm) but is structurally blind to a hardlink to a file *inside* a protected directory root. ADR-021 nowhere mentions hardlinks, though ADR-017 uses them as a first-class mechanism in Decision 7.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021 D4, `docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md:144-149`: "Every comparison is `os.SameFile` on resolved objects — never a string prefix (ADR-017:247-250, and the rejected alternative at :1348-1349: *\"Lexical prefix comparison fails under symlinks, APFS case behavior, and sibling prefixes\"*)." The cited rejected-alternative list names symlinks, APFS case, and sibling prefixes — not hardlinks.

ADR-021's test list, `:358-361`, tests only the symlink alias: "symlink aliases of a protected root reject (identity, not string)". No hardlink case.

That ADR-017 knows hardlinks are live in this system, `docs/adr/017-mount-authorization.md:361-362`: "Historical pipeline-trace entries are regular-file hardlinks governed only by Decision 7".

PROBE (`mise exec -- go run .../hard.go`, implementing all three D4 arms with real `os.Stat` on both sides — i.e. the *best case*, with finding #1 already repaired):
```
Case 1  hardlink -> ~/.netrc (a protected root ITSELF)
        D4 rejects = true   (S==P arm catches it: same inode)

Case 2  hardlink -> ~/.ssh/id_rsa (INSIDE protected dir ~/.ssh)
        S=.../home/src/project/notes2
        D4 rejects = false   <-- ESCAPE
        contents readable via alias: "PRIVATE KEY"
```
The blocked floor does not cover it either: the components of `/…/home/src/project/notes2` contain no `.ssh` and no `id_rsa*` match (`mc/boundary/blocked.go:29`, `:50`), and `BlockPolicy.Rejects` classifies "only address components; it never scans directory contents" (`blocked.go:96-97`).

For calibration, I also probed the aliasing routes D4 *does* settle, and it settles them correctly — these are not findings: `os.SameFile("/Users/vinchenkov", "/System/Volumes/Data/Users/vinchenkov") = true` (firmlinks preserve dev/ino), and `os.SameFile("/Users/vinchenkov", "/USERS/vinchenkov") = true` (APFS case-folding preserves dev/ino), confirming ADR-017:274's claim. Note `EvalSymlinks("/USERS/vinchenkov") = "/USERS/vinchenkov"` — the canonical *string* is not normalized, which is why the identity rule rather than the string is load-bearing.
raised.failure_scenario: Any host-side actor with traverse on `~/.ssh` and write on an allowlisted workspace creates `ln ~/.ssh/id_rsa ~/src/project/notes` (hardlink creation needs no read permission on the target). `~/src/project` is allowlisted; `~/src/project/notes` resolves to itself under `EvalSymlinks` (a hardlink is not a symlink), so `ResolveSource` yields a canonical path with no protected component. All three D4 arms miss: it is not `== ~/.ssh`, the ancestor walk from it never reaches `~/.ssh`, and it is not an ancestor of `~/.ssh`. The floor misses. The mount is authorized and the key bytes are readable in-container. Exploitability caveat, stated honestly: the model cannot create this link itself (it never has `~/.ssh` mounted), so this needs a pre-existing host-side link — it is a completeness gap in D4's claim, not a live model-reachable escape. But D4 is the document that pins the containment mechanism and asserts identity settles aliasing; it should either name hardlinks as out of scope with a rationale, or note that a hardlinked inode inside a protected directory is not reachable by any lineage walk.

raised.title: D8's "compare on that pair" reintroduces the lexical suffix comparison ADR-017 rejected, with no component grammar, no case rule, and no definition of the "ambiguity" that is supposed to deny
raised.claim: D8 resolves an absent protected member to (nearest existing canonical ancestor, unresolved suffix) and says to "compare on that pair", but specifies nothing about how the suffix half is compared. The suffix has no inode, so it can only be compared as a string — exactly the mechanism ADR-017:1348-1349 rejects and D4 bans. D8 does not notice that it is carving an exception out of D4's own rule, and "ambiguity denies" is stated three times without ever being defined, so an implementer has no way to know which comparisons must deny.
raised.evidence:
ADR-021 D8, `docs/adr/021-mount-jurisdiction.md:268-271`: "**`denied_paths` and any absent protected root**: resolve the nearest existing canonical ancestor, retain the unresolved suffix, and compare on that pair. **Ambiguity denies** — if it cannot be decided whether `S` intersects the would-be path, reject." That is the whole specification of the comparison.

D8's own framing of why this matters, `:265-266`: "You cannot `os.SameFile` a path with no inode, so this is an algorithm requirement, not a footnote." — correct, and then no algorithm is given for the half that has no inode.

Against D4's flat prohibition, `:146-147`: "Every comparison is `os.SameFile` on resolved objects — never a string prefix", and the rejected alternative it cites, `docs/adr/017-mount-authorization.md:1348-1349`: "Lexical prefix comparison fails under symlinks, APFS case behavior, and sibling prefixes."

The sibling-prefix trap ADR-017 names explicitly at `:273`: "`/safe-root-evil` never satisfies `/safe-root`."

The existing code shows the shape D8 would have to match and does not describe: `match()` derives the suffix from the identity walk and `Authorize` validates it component-wise via `ValidateTarget` (`mc/boundary/identity.go:256-264`, `:284-303`), and the ADR-017 rule it implements is "Derive the relative suffix from that same identity walk, never unchecked string-prefix arithmetic" (`:249-250`).
raised.failure_scenario: An implementer reads "compare on that pair" and writes `strings.HasPrefix(sourceSuffix, denySuffix)`. Deny path `~/ws/secret` does not exist; nearest existing ancestor `~/ws`, suffix `secret`. Source `~/ws/secret-evil` exists and is allowlisted. `HasPrefix("secret-evil", "secret")` is true, so a legitimate mount rejects with `mount.denied_root` — ADR-017:273's `/safe-root-evil` bug, reintroduced in the one place D4's identity rule cannot reach. I probed the opposite (under-blocking) direction and could not construct an escape: on case-insensitive APFS an absent deny path implies no case-variant exists, so no existing source can sit under it — which means this defect is fail-closed and is a correctness/false-rejection defect rather than a containment hole. It still needs fixing before TDD, because D8 is one of the two decisions with a "Tests that pin it" line (`:372-373`, "a non-existent deny path resolves through its nearest existing ancestor; ambiguity denies") that cannot be written against an undefined predicate. D8 should say: the suffix is compared component-wise against the suffix derived from the same identity walk, never as a byte prefix; state the case rule; and enumerate what "ambiguity" is (at minimum: the ancestor resolution itself hit a symlink, or the source's own suffix from that ancestor is a case-variant on a case-insensitive volume).
